implementation_summary: Added an actor proof-factory substrate that records explicit per-actor proof levels, failure classes, durable proof rows, proof summary/list/detail APIs, run-to-proof promotion, a resumable proof runner script, visible proof counters/status in the existing UI, and focused tests that prevent API-mapped actors from being claimed as live E2E proven. Added fixes preventing failed actor runs from being promoted to ui_route_passed, preventing zero-item completed runs from being promoted to runtime_smoke_passed, preventing the proof runner from asserting UI proof by default, routing generated test inputs to hosted first-party fixtures, and fixing the runtime family classifier so the word leading no longer triggers lead-generation routing. Post-deploy fix: non-product actor proof outputs now survive normalization, and the HTTP worker lazy-loads yt-dlp only for video URLs so review/contact/content actors are not coupled to optional video tooling. Native video actor support added: yt_dlp route_strategy now uses the existing YoutubeDlpConnector through a metadata-only provider SDK runner and is no longer blocked as unsupported. Proof runner rollout semantics hardened: status now reports latest proof per actor with raw row audit count, and --resume-success-only allows failed transport rows to be retried without re-running successful actors.
